observations.recommendation:
Ensure compliance with Sections 7.3.2 to 7.3.5 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 and:

•	Require each department/ end-user unit to carefully plan the procurement of common use supplies through proper forecasting, projections, and estimations of inventories to be used and stocked and accomplish the PPMP template before submitting the same to the Budget Officer for Budget approval and BAC Secretariat for consolidation;

•	Stop the practice of individual purchasing of supplies. Instead, purchase it in bulk in order to enjoy the benefits of trade discounts and also to avoid price fluctuations of purchasing the same supplies from different suppliers; and

•	Submit the Approved Annual Procurement Plan as reference in audit to facilitate review of procurement activities.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that:
The Inventory Committee assign a unique Property Number for each PPE item following the required code format in the numbering system as provided in Section 5 of the Circular.
For CY 2022, the Inventory Committee prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit the same to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in compliance with the pertinent provisions of Section 5 of the Circular.
Each office of the LGU fill up the Inventory Count Form and submit the same to the Property Unit for verification. The form will then be used by the Inventory Committee as basis in the preparation of its PIP and will be considered as part of their inventory working paper.
Moreover, the Inventory Committee record/document the physical count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major account group and will be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE after the physical count.
The Property Unit prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) during physical inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts.
The LCE ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended that the Management:
Prioritize the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit it to this office for verification.
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to reconcile the Cash in Bank account balances with the Treasurer’s cashbooks by determining the transactions that caused the net difference of ₱14,702,804.87.
For dormant bank accounts, revert the unused cash to the source agency or to the Bureau of Treasury.
Thereafter, conduct quarterly reconciliation of such balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected either by the Treasury or Accounting Office will be adjusted and posted in their respective books.

We recommended that the Management:
Revisit the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 to reinvigorate the agency on its duties and responsibilities on the accounting and reporting guidelines of local roads;
Prioritize the conduct of physical count of properties by directing the Accounting, Engineering, and GSO offices to coordinate with each other as regards the recording and reporting of local roads to ensure that local roads are properly tagged with their respective road network IDs and other technical details are properly noted; and finally
Direct the Municipal Accountant to include the details of local roads in the notes to financial statements.

We recommended that the Management:
Prepare adjusting entries to transfer the buildings and other structures in the trust fund to the general fund, and provide depreciation charges accordingly; and
Hasten the updating of PPE records and conduct a complete physical count of the Municipality’s properties in line with COA Circular 2020-006 about one time PPE cleansing.

We recommended that the Management strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 in the proper mode of procurement and stop the practice of splitting purchase requisitions or contracts and other related transactions to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process and to obtain a fair and equitable contract for the best interest of the government.

We recommended that the Management establish specific set of guidelines in the granting of livelihood assistance to its constituents to ensure efficient implementation of the program and fund utilization.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit justification/s for still not complying with our prior year’s audit recommendations.
Ensure compliance with Sections 7.3.2 to 7.3.5 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 and:
Require each department/ end-user unit to carefully plan and monitor the procurement of common use supplies through proper forecasting, projections, and estimations of inventories to be used and stocked and accomplish the PPMP template before submitting the same to the Budget Officer for Budget approval and BAC Secretariat for consolidation; and
Stop the practice of individual purchasing of supplies. Instead, with reference to the Annual Procurement Plan for common use supplies, purchase it in bulk in order to enjoy the benefits of trade discounts and also to avoid price fluctuations of purchasing the same supplies from different suppliers

We recommended that the Management direct the MSWDO in coordination with the Office of the Mayor to prepare and issue a written policy on the payment/grant of AICS in line with DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02 series of 2014 as amended by Memorandum Circular No. 15 series of 2014 to set forth the guidelines in strengthening and enhancing the implementation of Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situations (AICS) of the Municipality to ensure proper reporting, auditing and monitoring and uphold the delivery of services to its constituents.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit justification/s in resorting to Shopping after the failure of bidding contrary to Section 35.5 of RA 9184 in its procurement of the procurement of 22 units of Engine (6.5 HP);
Direct BAC or end user unit to be more proactive by adhering to the provision of Section 35.5 of RA 9184 before resorting to a procurement mode after failure of bidding in all its succeeding procurement transactions; and
Revisit the provisions of RA 9184 to fully comply with the procurement laws.
We recommended that the Management stop the practice of indicating brand names in the Purchase Request and adhere to the provision of Section 18 of RA 9184.
We recommended that the Management submit the lacking documents to support the veracity of the purchase to the Auditor for review.

We recommended that the Management:
Deduct the excess contract cost of ₱139,205.55 from the contractor’s claims.
Adhere to the provisions of DPWH D.O No. 197 series of 2016 in the preparation of the Approved Budget Cost in all of its projects.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Human Resource Office to submit a copy of the leave cards of the nine (9) LGU employees who availed the monetization as supporting attachments and thereafter, certify the leave application only if the leave credit balance is indicated.
Direct the Human Resource Office and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that Application for Leave supporting the payment of monetization of earned leave credits shall be completely signed and approved by proper official in accordance with COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended that the Management:
Ascertain and address the causes of the delay in the submission of transaction documents to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.
Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer shall ensure that the required reports, disbursement vouchers, official receipts and other pertinent documents are completely submitted to the Accounting Office not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month for the immediate recording in the books of accounts.
Further, the Municipal Accounting Office shall give priority to the timely preparation and submission of the transaction documents for audit in such form and within the period prescribed by the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Management direct BAC or end user unit to be more proactive by adhering to the provisions of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 and employ negotiated procurement – emergency purchase in the purchase of goods and materials to mitigate the negative impact of COVID-19.

We recommended that the Management strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and GPPB Circular No. 1-2020 dated April 6, 2020 in the proper modes of procurement and stop the practice of splitting purchase requisitions or contracts to expedite the procurement of highly needed supplies and materials at the most advantageous price to the government with the necessary safeguards and prevention of abuses and corruption in the procurement process

We recommended that the Management exercise diligence in complying with the documentary requirements for the cash advance of payroll as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001 to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

Submit justification/s for the failure of the LGU to comply with the documents required as stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement signed between the Municipality and DSWD on April 23, 2020.

Moving forward, ensure strict compliance with the roles and responsibilities required for the LGU in accordance with the stipulations set forth in the Memorandum of Agreement signed between the Municipality and other agencies.

For unreconciled PPE accounts, prioritize the conduct of inventory and reconciliation and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect their correct balances by providing depreciation charges to depreciable assets, request write-off and disposal for unserviceable properties.

Require the concerned officials to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within the period prescribed, which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

Require the Municipal Accountant to speed up the updating of PPE records in order to provide depreciation on the Municipality’s depreciable assets in accordance with Section 4(o) of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I
observations.managementAction: Updated the PPE records and provide depreciation

observations.recommendation:
Direct all revenue collectors to strictly remit their collections daily and intact, 

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Liquidating Officer to strictly monitor the remittance of collection daily, and 

Impose disciplinary actions to those committing the same violation of the regulation.
observations.managementAction: Collectors whose collections found to be delayed in remittance and deposits ask remit their collections on time and some were as of this time were temporarily suspended from holding official receipts.

observations.recommendation: procure commonly used supplies directly from the PS or its depots as required in Sections 1 and 4 of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011, if there be office supplies which are not available in the PS, it will then be purchased from the private suppliers in accordance with the Revised Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184.
observations.managementAction: Procure commonly used supplies from DBM- PS

observations.recommendation:
Submit documents showing that those who received the Mid-year and Year-end bonuses had met the conditions set forth in Budget Circular Nos.2016-3 and 2016-4.

Henceforth, ensure that the granting of the bonuses is in accordance with the DBM issuances to avoid excessive or unauthorized payments.
observations.managementAction: Furnish the resident auditor the copy of individual IPCRs on the period covered to attest the basis for availment of the said bonus

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents to support the RATA granted to officials and employees in CY 2016.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the disbursements for RATA to the Officials and Department Heads of the Municipality are supported with complete documentation as required in Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Sections 1.1.1, 1.2.1, and 5.2 of COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Already complied

observations.recommendation: instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the above-mentioned lacking documents and ensure that payment for wages of job order workers are supported with complete documents to establish the propriety of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Payments to Jos were supported with the required documents.

observations.recommendation:
Strictly comply to the provisions of Republic Act 9003 in proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste. 

Ensure the implementation/enforcement of the programs, projects and activities embodied in the Municipality’s ESWM Plan pursuant to Republic Act 9003.

Monitor the implementation of the ESWM plan of the Municipality and adopt measures to promote and ensure its viability and effective implementation.
observations.managementAction: Comply with the provisions of RA 9003.

observations.recommendation:
Implement appropriate methods to promote timely conduct of the procurement process of all priority development projects, with emphasis on Section 7.6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.  

Ensure that planned developmental programs/projects/activities are promptly and timely implemented

instruct the GAD Secretariat to exert more effort in the establishment and maintenance of a GAD database containing all sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics to comply with the provisions of PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.
observations.managementAction: Direct the GAD Focal person to consolidate the GAD database
No action taken.
observations.reason: Property stock cards were still not prepared and the annual physical count of PPE was still not conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all depository bank accounts of the LGU within ten (10) days from receipt of the Bank Statements and submit the same together with the Bank Statements and paid checks to the Audit Team, and hire qualified personnel who will assist the Municipal Accountant in the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statement.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: the municipality still failed to prepare and submit the required Bank Reconciliation Statements in CY 2016

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall exert effort to compel the Accountable Officers to settle their unliquidated cash advances. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to review the prior years’ unliquidated balance which were without specified Accountable Officers and make the necessary adjustment, if any. Further, no additional cash advance should be granted to those who have existing unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: There were still unliquidated cash advances as of December 8, 2016,. (Still waiting for the report on unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2016)

observations.recommendation: Management shall hire additional personnel to augment the manpower in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and of the Municipal Accounting Office and/or employ measure to ensure timely submission of accounts and financial reports pursuant to Section 39 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 to facilitate timely review/audit thereof by the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: There were still delays noted in the municipality’s submission of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairperson direct the BAC Secretariat to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report of the Municipality and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format   within fourteen calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance to Section 12.2 of Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat still failed to submit the required Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPPB in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 119 and 124 of the NGAS, Manual in the maintenance of property records and in the submission of the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).

We recommend and  Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to speed up the updating of PPE records in order to provide depreciation on the Municipality’s depreciable assets in accordance with Section 4(o) of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I

We recommended that the Management shall:
Direct all revenue collectors to strictly remit their collections daily and intact,
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Liquidating Officer to strictly monitor the remittance of collection daily, and
Impose disciplinary actions to those committing the same violation of the regulation.

We recommended and Management agreed to procure commonly used supplies directly from the PS or its depots as required in Sections 1 and 4 of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011, if there be office supplies which are not available in the PS, it will then be purchased from the private suppliers in accordance with the Revised Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Submit documents showing that those who received the Mid-year and Year-end bonuses had met the conditions set forth in Budget Circular Nos.2016-3 and 2016-4.
Henceforth, ensure that the granting of the bonuses is in accordance with the DBM issuances to avoid excessive or unauthorized payments.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents to support the RATA granted to officials and employees in CY 2016.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the disbursements for RATA to the Officials and Department Heads of the Municipality are supported with complete documentation as required in Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Sections 1.1.1, 1.2.1, and 5.2 of COA Circular 2012-001.
Management commented that they will comply with the audit recommendations and will also revise their RATA certification that will state that the official/employee did not use any government vehicle, and is not assigned any.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the above-mentioned lacking documents and ensure that payment for wages of job order workers are supported with complete documents to establish the propriety of the transactions.
Management commented that they will comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman instruct the BAC Secretariat to submit the lacking supporting documents as enumerated in Annex I hereof.
For future contracts entered into by the Municipality, direct the BAC Secretariat to submit the approved contracts together with the documentary requirements mentioned in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on time to facilitate the timely review and communication of deficiencies, if any.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat to strictly comply with Section 12.2 of Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the preparation and submission of the PMR to GPPB to properly inform the public of its procurement activities.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly ensure that the financial documents are submitted in accordance to Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Also, require the Accountant to monitor the work of his staff to hasten the submission of financial reports.
Designate additional employee, if possible, to help in submission of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents.

Management shall direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the disbursements for RATA to the Officials and Department Heads of the Municipality are (a) supported with complete documentation as required in Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Sections 1.1.1, 1.2.1, and 5.2 of COA Circular 2012-001, and completely signed by the proper officials as mandated in Section 4(5) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Attach Certifications of RATA claims
observations.reason: Verification revealed that Department Heads’ and Sanggunian Bayan Members’ certification did not state that employee/official did not use any government vehicle and was not assigned any vehicle.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management shall ensure that the Auditor is furnished with the copy of the contracts entered into by the Municipality within five (5) working days from its execution and shall be supported with complete documents as required in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Furnish the auditor with a copy of the contract within five days from its execution
observations.reason: The contracts were still not submitted on time but there were contracts which were supported with complete documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to deduct the excess contract cost of ₱128,640.00 from the claims of 3C’SL Construction and Supply for the Rehabilitation/Enhancement of Poblacion 1 Flood Control (Shoreline Protection). Henceforth, the LGU shall adhere to DPWH D.O No. 22 series of 2015 in the preparation of the ABC in all of its projects.
observations.managementAction: Deduct the excess contract cost.
observations.reason: The excess contract cost was already deducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management direct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that the provisions in RA 9184 and its revised IRR are strictly adhered in all of the procurement process of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Conducted bids in accordance with the IRR of RA 9184
observations.reason: Verification disclosed there were still minor deficiencies noted in the procurement process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert more effort in the establishment and maintenance of a GAD database containing all sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics to comply with the provisions of PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.
observations.managementAction: Gather data related to GAD to establish database.
observations.reason: The GAD Focal Point was in the process of consolidating the data gathered

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure strict compliance with RA 10121 and other rules and regulations on the creation and composition of MDRRMO through the enactment of a Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance, as required, with due consideration on the Personnel Services (PS) limitation and availability of funds and Department of Budget and Management (DBM) approval of the position.
observations.managementAction: Create MDRRMO position.
observations.reason: The application for the DRRMO position was still on hold at the Civil Service Commission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly follow the documentary requirements for government transactions provided under COA Circular No. 2012-001, particularly on the payment of salaries and wages of Job Orders.
observations.managementAction: Direct the job orders to attach accomplishment reports and DTRs when filing for payroll claims
observations.reason: Verification disclosed that   some payrolls for wages of Job Order workers of the municipality were not supported with the required accomplishment report. Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management implement the collection of non-recyclable materials and special wastes in the barangays; Separate containers/ receptacles  for all types of wastes shall be maintained from all sources; these containers must be properly marked or identified for on-site collections such as  compostable, non- recyclable, recyclable,  special waste and/or any other classification as may be determined by the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board; provide and maintain receptacles and Materials Recovery Facility for temporary placement of solid wastes; and provide for a controlled dump site.
observations.managementAction: Install Material Recovery Facilities in the Municipality and Barangays.
observations.reason: Material Recovery Facility were installed in the Barangays but collection of residual and special wastes was not done due to absence of Municipal dumpsite.
